zh.title: Solana/美元技术分析：偏弱震荡下行
zh.content:
Solana当前处于短期回调结构，价格运行在20日均线72下方，整体趋势偏弱，呈震荡下行格局，空头占优但存在反弹需求

技术指标显示MACD处于负值区域且动能线向下，空头动能延续，短期承压明显延续中

下方支撑关注68.08附近前低区域，上方阻力位于70.03及72日均线附近形成压制

操作建议以反弹高空为主，若突破70并站稳可轻仓试多，否则等待回调确认

en.title: Solana vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
SOL/USD remains under short-term bearish pressure after slipping to 68.77, trading below the 20-period simple moving average at 72.017. The recent decline of 1.64% over the past 24 hours, combined with an intraday range between 68.08 and 70.03, suggests sellers continue to control momentum despite occasional buying interest. Unless price reclaims the moving average, the prevailing trend is likely to remain cautious, with rallies potentially facing renewed selling pressure.

Technical indicators reinforce the current weakness, although the absence of an RSI reading limits confirmation of momentum extremes. The MACD value of -0.90632724 indicates negative momentum remains in place, implying bearish forces continue to outweigh bullish participation. With no signal line available, crossover analysis is unavailable, but the negative MACD alone suggests traders should remain alert for further downside unless momentum begins to stabilize through higher lows and stronger buying volume.

Immediate support is located near the session low around 68.08, with a sustained break below that level potentially exposing additional downside toward the psychological 67.00 region. On the upside, initial resistance appears near 70.03, while the more significant barrier remains the 20-day SMA at 72.017. A decisive move above these resistance levels would improve the short-term outlook and could encourage buyers to target higher recovery zones in subsequent trading sessions.

From a trading perspective, conservative participants may prefer waiting for confirmation before establishing new long positions. A successful rebound from support accompanied by improving momentum would provide a stronger technical foundation than attempting to catch a falling market. Short-term traders may continue favoring range-based strategies while price remains between support and resistance, whereas disciplined risk management with predefined stop-loss levels remains essential given the prevailing bearish bias and the possibility of heightened volatility.
